e2e.summary: All 18 responsive E2E tests passed across chromium, firefox, and webkit (6 viewports each: iphone-se, iphone-11, ipad, ipad-land, laptop, desktop). No horizontal overflow, no fatal console errors, hero heading visible across all viewports. Run completed in ~35.6s against production server on port 3200.

recommendations: Reduce unused JavaScript (~110ms savings on desktop)
Eliminate render-blocking resources (~4ms savings, score 0.5)
Add valid source maps for large first-party JavaScript (currently missing)
Reduce total byte weight of network payloads (score 0.5)
Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers (score 0.5)
Minimize main-thread work
Reduce JavaScript execution time
Avoid enormous network payloads
Reduce unused JavaScript
Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers

issues: 'CORE // ONLINE' bleeds/overlaps with footer link text on contact section
Experience header text overlapping (DEV.05 / SYS.TIMELINE crowded)
Contact heading not visible — fade timing
Contact heading not visible in screenshot due to fade timing (no truncation regression)
Contact heading not visible in screenshot due to fade timing (no truncation regression)

Contact heading 'PING // THE_CORE' not visible in screenshot — appears the new block-level fade has opacity 0 at scroll-snapshot time (animation didn't trigger). Truncation bug 'ING // THE_CORE' is GONE — no character clipping observed
Projects/Experience screenshots show only orb (cards below the section header are not in initial scroll viewport — same behavior as iter4, not new)
Contact heading not visible (same fade timing issue) — but no truncation
Hero: small orb dot still touches FRONTEND DEVELOPER badge edge (very minor)
Contact heading not visible in scroll snapshot — fade timing

instructions: Convergence rule: lhDesktop.performance >= targetDesktopPerf AND lhMobile.performance >= targetMobilePerf AND e2e.failed === 0 AND visual.overallScore >= 80.
If E2E failed > 0, list the specific viewport+browser combos that failed and a concrete fix per failure.
If lhDesktop or lhMobile under target, list the top 5 LH opportunities (e.g., "reduce unused JS", "minimize main-thread work") with file paths if known.
If visual issues exist, list them with viewport + section + suggested fix.
Return JSON { converged:bool, breakdown:{desktopPerf, mobilePerf, e2eRate, visual}, blockedBy:string[], recommendations:[{priority, category, file?, action}], summary:"..." }
prompt.role: QA lead synthesizing perf + responsive scores
prompt.task: Decide if we have converged. If not, produce a prioritized recommendation list for the next iteration.
